title: Luffy Declares His Dream After Defeating Kuro
description: On pages 2–5, the Syrup Village arc settles after Kuro’s defeat, focusing on Usopp’s decision, Kaya’s gratitude, and the end of the Usopp Pirates.
scene: The village chooses peace and secrecy while Usopp prepares to leave, turning his old lies into the beginning of a real journey.

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: He did it, Captain Kuro was...

dialogue.speaker: Kuro
dialogue.text: Even a ship of Marines weren't able to defeat the "100 plans Kuro"

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: There's no way I'd lose to a pirate who gives up his name and runs from the sea

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: The only time a pirate should give up his name is when he dies!!

dialogue.speaker: Kaya
dialogue.text: You better try to remember my name...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm the one who's going to become...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I guess even you can't stand up after being cut so many times

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Why did you get so mad earlier?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's OBVI- OUSLY because they're pirates

summary: This part gives Syrup Village an emotional farewell: Usopp protects the village’s peace, says goodbye to his friends, and steps toward becoming a real pirate.
